title: Tiberian Factions for Red Alert
description:
[i]My son asked me who would win if GDI battled the Soviets. Now we can find out.[/i]

[h2]Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] Adds GDI and Nod alongside the Allies and Soviets with full TD logic. Ion cannons, flame tanks, stealth tanks, Nod Airstrip, Refinery docking - all work.
[*] Full TD ground rosters plus navies (GDI Naval Yard: gunboat, destroyer, cruiser; Nod Sub Pen: attack and missile subs) and air power (GDI Airfield with A-10 bombing runs, Orca, Apache).
[*] Superweapons and support powers: Ion Cannon, Nuclear Strike, GDI GPS satellite, Nod spy plane and paratroopers.
[*] Nod Stealth Generator: a cloaking field that hides your base, with the generator itself as the visible weak point. Plus the anti-infantry Flame Bunker.
[*] Full Tiberium ecosystem and 31 TD skirmish maps. (The first time you load the mod you will need to run a skirmish match for the maps to appear in the Custom tab).
[*] GDI / Nod skirmish AI builds bases, economies, armies and air power, and scales its air force to the strongest air opponent.
[*] UI shows GDI / Nod logos on Skirmish select screen. TD EVA, unit voices, building and weapon sounds. Skirmish music mixes RA and TD tracks.
[*] Attack-move: hold Shift and click to advance while attacking enemies on the way.
[*] Quality of life features: rally points, A* pathfinding, smarter harvesters, repair bay queueing and extra zoom levels (adapted from CFE Patch Redux).
[/list]

[h2]Changelog[/h2]
[b]Version 4.1.0[/b]
[list]
[*] Separated tech trees. GDI, Nod, Allies and Soviets each build their own Construction Yard, MCV, War Factory and Helipad as distinct types. Capture one of these and you get that faction's full arsenal.
[*] Prerequisite policy locked down: only low tier infrastructure (power, refinery, repair) is shared across factions. Barracks, war factories, helipads, naval yards, airfields, radar and tech centres are faction identity and no longer cross the divide.
[*] Faction badges on the cameos once you build from more than one faction: capture a rival construction yard or war factory and those cameos show which of your factions makes each entry. The sidebar also sorts into faction blocks, shared buildables first, then Allied, Soviet, GDI and Nod, each in tech order.
[*] Unholy Alliance, a new entry in the lobby Mode dropdown that starts every player, human and AI, with all four factions' construction yards at once.
[*] Per slot AI difficulty. Each AI now takes its own Easy, Medium or Hard pick from the lobby, in skirmish and LAN alike, instead of falling back to all Hard.
[*] AI air power is back. The AI builds air production once its ground economy is established and fields helicopters and planes again.
[*] Smarter AI base logic: scouting under fair fog, a fix for Nod Temple and Stealth Generator build starvation, tier 2 buildings held behind an established economy, and harvesters that retreat home when idle.
[*] Classic graphics mode is locked out. The mod is HD only, so its content can no longer be dropped into a renderer it has no art for.
[*] Skirmish music rotation expanded to 107 tracks.
[/list]
[b][u]Version 4.0.0[/u][/b]
[list]
[*] Navies for GDI and Nod: GDI Naval Yard with gunboat, destroyer and cruiser (cruiser needs the Advanced Comm Center); Nod Sub Pen with attack subs and the Temple-guarded Missile Sub. Fleet and buildings carry faction-correct sidebar names.
[*] GDI Airfield and the A-10 Warthog, flying TD-authentic napalm bombing runs.
[*] Nod Stealth Generator: cloaks nearby friendly buildings and units.
[*] Nod Flame Bunker: anti-infantry flame emplacement.
[*] Support powers: GDI GPS satellite, Nod spy plane and paratroopers.
[*] Balance pass across all four factions: air, armor, defences, infantry and artillery ranges, plus GDI/Nod MCV and Construction Yard at RA parity.
[/list]
[b][u]Version 3.0.0[/u][/b]
[list]
[*] The harvester economy overhaul: cross-faction refinery docking (and capturing a refinery takes the harvester docked at it), visible unloading, dock times roughly halved and equalised across every harvester and refinery pairing, plus smarter field choice, enemy-ore avoidance and anti-stuck recovery.
[/list]
[b]Versions 2.0 to 2.4[/b]
[list]
[*] Tiberium ecosystem, 31-map TD map pack in HD (temperate, winter, desert), attack-move, A* pathfinding, cooperative chokepoint traffic, group-move fan-out, infantry Tiberium aversion, harvester recovery and self-repair.
[/list]
For the complete changelog (all versions), see GitHub:
https://github.com/gibbo101/cnc-ra-tiberian-factions/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

[h2]Planned[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Smarter AI[/b]: a dedicated AI improvement pass is next, covering build-order, defence, and how the AI uses its armies, navies, and superweapons.
[*] [b]Smarter unit movement[/b]: further cooperative traffic handling for the tightest vehicle-vs-vehicle gaps. Plus the next wave of CFE Patch Redux adoptions (this is a DLL mod, so CFE Patch itself can't run alongside it).
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*] This is a [b]DLL mod[/b]. It will not work alongside any other mod that also modifies RedAlert.dll. Disable other DLL mods such as CFE Patch before enabling this one.
[*] Single-player skirmish tested. LAN does work but I have experienced crashes with crates and have not yet investigated this. Recommend turning crates off for LAN play.
[/list]

[h2]Known limitations[/h2]
[list]
[*] The [b]Deploy[/b] keyboard shortcut no longer works for any faction's MCV, now that all four factions field their own MCV type. This is a limitation in the game's launcher that a mod cannot reach. To deploy an MCV, select it and click it: the deploy cursor still works. The [b]Select-all-combat-units (A)[/b] key will also grab harvesters and MCVs, so use a drag-box to select your army instead.
[/list]

[h2]Source and licensing[/h2]
GitHub: https://github.com/gibbo101/cnc-ra-tiberian-factions
Built on top of [url=https://github.com/TheAssemblyArmada/Vanilla-Conquer]Vanilla Conquer[/url]. DLL source is [b]GPL v3[/b], inherited from EA's 2020 Remastered Collection source release.

[h2]Acknowledgements and inspiration[/h2]
This project doesn't bundle these mods, but their work shaped how we approached the engine. Thanks to:
[list]
[*] [b]Reilsss[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2853520457]Reilsss's Command & Conquer in Red Alert[/url], inspiration for reimagining Red Alert's factions as GDI and Nod, and for how to bring TD's sounds across.
[*] [b]DontCryJustDie[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3003163891]TD-Assets[/url], TD art and audio surfaced into the RA engine.
[*] [b]JohnnyJigglez[/b]: [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/commandandconquerremastered/mods/21]EMC (Enhanced Modding Capabilities)[/url], INI-driven extensibility reference.
[*] [b]Bast75[/b] and [b]xXMini FrankiXx[/b]: skirmish AI ideas that shaped the 4.1 AI pass.
[*] [b]ChthonVII[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2268301299]CFE Patch Redux[/url] (with cfehunter and Root-Core), source of the 2.1.0 rally points, harvester, repair bay and zoom features (GPL v3).
[/list]

Not endorsed by or affiliated with Electronic Arts.
